Principles of Management

Chapter 1 — Foundations of Management

Interactive Web-Based Independent Learning

0 of 5 sections complete

What is management?

Management is the process of planning, organising, leading and controlling organisational resources to achieve goals effectively and efficiently.

Effectiveness

Doing the right things

Focuses on achieving the correct goals. A café that hits its 20% sales target = effective. Ask: Did we achieve our goals?

Efficiency

Doing things right

Focuses on wise use of resources — minimising waste, cost and time. Ask: Did we use resources well?

Good managers need BOTH

The 6 organisational resources

Human  ·  Physical  ·  Financial  ·  Information  ·  Technology  ·  Materials / Raw materials

Manager vs Leader

Two roles, one person

Manager: plans, organises, controls.  Leader: inspires, motivates, influences. A good manager should also be a good leader.

Management in Malaysia

Happens everywhere

Cafés & restaurants  ·  Hospitals & clinics  ·  Universities & colleges  ·  SMEs & retail shops  ·  Online sellers / Shopee  ·  Government agencies

The POLC management cycle

A continuous cycle — after controlling, managers go back to planning for improvement.

📋

1. Planning

Set goals and decide actions to achieve them. Strategic, tactical or operational.

🏗️

2. Organising

Arrange people, resources and tasks to execute the plan. Includes structure and delegation.

💡

3. Leading

Guide, motivate and influence employees to work toward goals.

📊

4. Controlling

Monitor results, compare with goals, and take corrective action.

Levels of Management

Top → strategic decisions  |  Middle → coordinate departments  |  First-line → supervise daily work

Top managers — Conceptual skillVery high
Middle managers — Human skillHigh
First-line — Technical skillVery high
Henri Fayol's 14 Principles (selected)

Division of Work  ·  Authority  ·  Discipline  ·  Unity of Command  ·  Unity of Direction  ·  Equity  ·  Initiative  ·  Esprit de Corps
